channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iran defies international pressure, increasing its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium, UN says
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Iran has defied international demands to rein in its nuclear program and has increased its stockpile of uranium enriched to near weapons-grade levels, according to a confidential report by the United Nations’ nuclear watchdog seen Tuesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine fires several US-made longer-range missiles into Russia for the first time
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine fired several American-supplied longer-range missiles into Russia, officials said Tuesday, marking the first time Kyiv used the weapons that way in 1,000 days of war.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Italy recovers Etruscan artifacts worth $8.5 billion bound for black market
item.description: Italian authorities have announced the seizure of an illegal excavation of an Etruscan burial site in the central Umbria region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: School closures and travel delays as Arctic air brings snow and sleet to parts of the UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Dozens of schools closed and road and train travel ground to a halt in many parts of the U.K. on Tuesday as a cold snap brought snow, ice and sleet across the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: World's hottest calendar drops 2025 edition, proceeds going to Hurricane Helene, Milton efforts
item.description: This year, the funds collected from calendar sales will support those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, to provide immediate relief and aid to impacted communities.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: On board with Senegal’s navy as it searches for migrants on a popular but deadly route toward Europe
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — As dawn arrived off the coast of Senegal, navy commander Assane Seye scanned the horizon.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE RISEMBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin lowers threshold for Russian nuclear weapons use after Biden's Ukraine missile decision
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Tuesday lowered the threshold for nuclear weapons use, just two days after the U.S. authorized Ukraine to use long-range missiles inside of Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Putin signs revised doctrine lowering threshold for nuclear response if Russia is attacked
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a revised nuclear doctrine stating that any attack on Russia supported by a country with nuclear power could potentially trigger a nuclear response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US envoy says Israel-Hezbollah truce is 'within our grasp' as Gaza food crisis worsens after looting
item.description: US envoy Amos Hochstein says an agreement to end the Israel-Hezbollah war is “within our grasp” after meeting with officials in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb, Wafaa Shurafa And Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 45 pro-democracy activists get 4 to 10 years in prison in Hong Kong's biggest national security case
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Forty-five ex-lawmakers and activists were sentenced to four to 10 years in prison Tuesday in Hong Kong’s biggest national security case under a Beijing-imposed law that crushed a once-thriving pro-democracy movement.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG and ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Concerns grow for imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi's health in Iran
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi underwent a complex surgery in Iran that saw part of a bone in her right leg removed over cancer fears but was immediately returned to prison, raising the risks to her life, rights groups warned.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK farmers plan to protest at Parliament over a tax hike they say will ruin family farms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — With banners, bullhorns, toy tractors and an angry message, British farmers are descending on Parliament on Tuesday to protest a hike in inheritance tax that they say will deal a “hammer blow” to struggling family farms. U.K.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moscow warns the US over allowing Ukraine to hit Russian soil with longer-range weapons
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Kremlin warned Monday that President Joe Biden’s decision to let Ukraine strike targets inside Russia with U.S.-supplied longer-range missiles adds “fuel to the fire” of the war and would escalate international tensions even higher.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abuse survivors urge the Vatican to globalize the zero-tolerance policy it approved in the US
item.description: ROME (AP) — Survivors of clergy sexual abuse urged the Vatican on Monday to expand its zero-tolerance policy that it approved for the U.S. Catholic Church in 2002 to the rest of the world, arguing that children everywhere should be protected from predator priests. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said that Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s choice for director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: India has overtaken China as the top source of international students for U.S. colleges, according to a report from Open Doors. For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America, up to 331,602.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Hungarian official criticizes Biden decision allowing Ukraine to use US weapons to strike Russia
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary's foreign minister lashed out on Monday at U.S. President Joe Biden's decision to authorize Ukraine to use missiles supplied by Washington to strike deeper inside Russia, saying the move could serve to escalate the war and threaten the outbreak of a global conflict.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike kills Hezbollah spokesman in Beirut; at least 30 dead in Gaza airstrikes
item.description: A rare Israeli strike in central Beirut has killed the chief spokesman for the Hezbollah militant group. It was the latest in targeted killings of senior Hezbollah officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Negotiators are urged to get down to business as climate talks in Baku enter second week
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — United Nations climate talks resumed Monday with negotiators urged to make progress on a stalled-out deal that could see developing countries get more money to spend on clean energy and adapting to climate-charged weather extremes. U.N.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, MELINA WALLING and SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bevy of immigration programs at risk under Trump mass deportation plan
item.description: Immigrants with humanitarian or temporary legal status in the United States are at risk of being rendered effectively undocumented by the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Trump compiles his pro-Israel team: Here are the key players
item.description: President-elect Trump is compiling one of the most pro-Israel teams of any administration in history, following a presidential battle that saw Vice President Harris lose support from voters upset the Biden administration armed Israel in its war against Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Russia launched more than 200 missiles, drones at Ukrainian infrastructure: Zelensky
item.description: On Sunday, Russia launched a drone and missile attack on Ukraine in what’s being seen as the largest barrage in recent months, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: T-Mobile hacked in massive Chinese cyber-espionage: Wall St. Journal
item.description: It was part of a campaign to spy on the phones of high-value intelligence targets, the Journal reports.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Protesters storm Putin-backed parliament in Georgia breakaway region
item.description: Protesters in the separatist region of Abkhazia, Georgia stormed the parliament building to demand the region's president step down and reject an investment agreement that could benefit wealthy Russians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Priest stabbed in the face during Mass as religion-based hate crime is on the rise worldwide
item.description: A priest stabbed in the face during Mass is one of the most recent cases of a religion-based hate crime, which is one the rise around the globe, though Jews are most often victims of the attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driver rams his car into crowd in China, killing 35. Police say he was upset about his divorce
item.description: ZHUHAI, China (AP) — A man who authorities said was upset over his divorce settlement rammed his car into a crowd of people exercising at a sports complex in southern China, killing 35 and severely injuring dozens of others, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: NG HAN GUAN and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Church of England head Justin Welby resigns over handling of sex abuse scandal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Archbishop of Canterbury Justin Welby, head of the Church of England and spiritual leader of the global Anglican Communion, resigned Tuesday after an investigation found that he failed to tell police about serial physical and sexual abuse by a volunteer at Christian summer camps as soo
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jury awards Abu Ghraib detainees $42 million, holds contractor responsible
item.description: ALEXANDRIA, Va. (AP) — A U.S. jury on Tuesday awarded $42 million to three former detainees of Iraq's notorious Abu Ghraib prison, holding a Virginia-based military contractor responsible for contributing to their torture and mistreatment two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT, Associated Press, NewsNation
